mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS transmits lower discount rates and easier financial conditions to equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating, because easing without an inflation shock preserves real-rate relief.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below baseline in more than 60% of months with falling FEDFUNDS, or if the signal loses predictive value when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above a recent-trend threshold reflects labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which compresses expected earnings and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns on average, or if positive returns occur in more than 40% of instances after a 0.3 pp 3-month rise in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in GDPC1, RSAFS, and HOUST indicates broad demand acceleration that supports revenue growth and cyclical equity performance, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-proxy signal does not outperform each single proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if positive 60-day forward returns fail to dominate when all three series are improving together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO relative to a weak labor backdrop signals supply-side recovery and higher utilization without immediate wage pressure, which supports equity margins and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive months while UNRATE is flat or falling
failure_condition: Reject if months meeting the joint condition do not show positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE is rising alongside INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero, an additional decline in CPIAUCSL increases the real policy gap and eases bond-equity discounting, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.50% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month inflation declines
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and disinflation condition is not followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if returns are not materially better than when only one of the two conditions holds.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in RSAFS after prior strength signals weakening final demand and lower near-term revenue growth, which should pressure equity prices over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if negative 3-month annualized RSAFS growth is not associated with below-average 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips positive in most triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS series combined with rising UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market contraction that reduces aggregate income and spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines for 2 months and UNRATE rises simultaneously
failure_condition: Reject if the joint contraction signal is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in most cases, or if PAYEMS declines alone explain the effect without UNRATE confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of weak industrial production, a new rise in HOUST marks stabilization in rate-sensitive activity and a turn away from recessionary impulse, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is below its recent level and HOUST turns up after a prior decline
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST upturns during weak INDPRO do not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent outside stabilization phases.
